title: C ABI declarations broke alignment and multiline function rendering
summary: The frontend's width and function helpers encoded C++ formatter assumptions that failed on ordinary C structs and parameter lists with several arguments on one continuation row.
details: Bare uninitialized scalar fields were rendered as no_init name: type but excluded from field_dims, so alignment either did not run or formed pointer-only islands inside one struct.

flip_param_line selected only the first parameter_declaration beginning on a continuation row. Later parameters stayed raw while the first colon was padded against their later source columns, producing a huge gap and a mixed grammar.

The C-style return mover measured the parameter close column against the opening declarator row. For a multiline declaration this made the injection point invalid, leaving a prefix-stripped leading status type instead of def and -> status.
field_dims now mirrors the renderer's bare member/global acceptance gate, so all displayed fields in a contiguous declaration run share one width model.
The multiline parameter path now flips every complete parameter on the row and disables vertical colon padding for an entire list if any row has more than one parameter.
C-style function layout is computed once per changedtick from Tree-sitter and indexed by the type/name/close rows; each row owns only its extmarks, preserving row-scoped cursor and Visual reveal.

provenance.reference: conversation 2026-07-11 qnpeps.h lines 23-112 and permute_axes overloads
provenance.note: Owner supplied three concrete failures: unaligned Qnpeps structs, a catastrophically padded packed continuation row, and wrapped CUDA overload parameters.
The exact header was captured through Neovim ext_linegrid before and after the fix; no file in the thesis repository was modified.
The three formerly divergent width/ownership assumptions are corrected at their generic structured owners.

evidence.reference: tests/frontend_interaction_spec.lua C ABI and overflow-parameter blocks
evidence.claim: Bare and pointer fields align together, every packed parameter flips, concrete returns move across rows, both permute_axes layouts render completely, reveal restores independently, and source bytes remain unchanged.

evidence.reference: real ext-linegrid capture of qnpeps.h lines 23-123
evidence.claim: The complete affected header region renders consistently after the generic correction, including later rangefinder declarations.

questions: Why did qnpeps_ctx_build_dlenv render with a huge gap and raw trailing parameters?
Why did bare Qnpeps C struct fields fail to align?
How are concrete return types moved when the closing parenthesis is on another row?

last_verified.method: Exact read-only source capture, AST/ownership diagnosis, deterministic C/CUDA interaction fixtures, and full-header ext-linegrid inspection.
